key_insight: The paper proves that gradient descent can converge near a stationary point for broad neural network architectures beyond the NTK regime by using trajectory-dependent analytic and smoothness arguments.
engineering_relevance: This gives a more realistic theory for training modern architectures like pre-normalized transformers, and suggests learning-rate scaling should depend on depth and bottleneck dimensions rather than just width.
method: Builds an iterate-dependent PL-type inequality using analyticity and measure-zero arguments.
Proves Lipschitz smoothness along the GD path via polynomial generalized smoothness and a local relaxed dissipative condition.
Derives scaling and structural implications under Xavier initialization and residual/compositional architectures.
evidence_signal: Theoretical analysis only: the abstract reports proof of convergence for almost all initializations, not an empirical benchmark study.
watch_item: The guarantees are asymptotic and neighborhood-based, so practical performance may still depend on architecture-specific constants and assumptions that are hard to verify.
paper_title: Convergence of Gradient Descent for General Neural Network Architectures Beyond the NTK Regime
